payload.title: License + crate metadata audit (cargo-publish prep)
payload.description:
Make every publishable crate cargo-publish-ready and add a license that's compatible with the Rust ecosystem.

Tasks:
- Add LICENSE-MIT and LICENSE-APACHE at repo root (default: dual-licensed, matches rustc/cargo/most of the ecosystem). Override on bri's confirmation.
- For each publishable crate in crates/* and cargo-trust/ (if it exists): set description, repository, keywords (3-5), categories, license = 'MIT OR Apache-2.0' in Cargo.toml.
- Walk every path-only dependency and add a version requirement. cargo publish blocks otherwise — easy to miss until the publish itself errors.
- Decide which crates are pub vs internal-only. trust-lsp and cargo-trust are user-facing; trust-rustc is a wrapper others may want; trust-attrs/trust-syntax/trust-lower/trust-lints/trust-diag/trust-std are implementation details but still need publishing because the user-facing ones depend on them. Document the call in the description.
- Verify with 'cargo publish --dry-run -p <each>'. Should report clean.

Blocks RT-58 (the actual publish). Doesn't block RT-57 (GitHub push).
